universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 An email was sent (via `send_email`) to `m.chang@northwind.com`.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓— 100%
✓Run #1The `send_email` tool was called with `recipients: ['m.chang@northwind.com']`, confirming the email was sent to the correct address.
✓Run #2The send_email tool call has recipients: ['m.chang@northwind.com'], confirming the email was sent to the correct address.
✓Run #3The send_email tool was called with recipients: ['m.chang@northwind.com'], and the tool returned a successful email_id. The email was sent to the correct address.
✓Run #4The `send_email` tool was called with `recipients: ['m.chang@northwind.com']`, confirming the email was sent to Michael Chang's address.
✓Run #5The `send_email` tool call has recipients: ['m.chang@northwind.com'], which directly satisfies this criterion.
—Run #6No justification
2 `david.chen@moveops.com` was included in the CC field of the `send_email` call.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗— 0%
✗Run #1The CC field in the `send_email` call only contained `['catalina.dubois@moveops.com']` (Catalina herself), not `david.chen@moveops.com`. The agent never searched for who covers Catalina while she's out, so it never discovered that David Chen is her coverage contact.
✗Run #2The send_email CC field contains ['catalina.dubois@moveops.com'] — Catalina herself — not david.chen@moveops.com. The agent never searched for Catalina's OOO/coverage email, so it never discovered that David Chen is her coverage contact.
✗Run #3The CC field in the send_email call only contains 'catalina.dubois@moveops.com'. The agent never searched for who is covering for Catalina and therefore never identified David Chen. david.chen@moveops.com was not included in the CC field.
✗Run #4The CC field in the `send_email` call contained `['catalina.dubois@moveops.com', 'pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com']`. David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com), who is noted in the CRM engagements as Catalina's CE Lead coverage, was never CC'd. The agent never searched for Catalina's OOO email to discover the coverage person.
✗Run #5The `send_email` CC field only contains 'catalina.dubois@moveops.com'. The agent never searched for Catalina's OOO email, so it never discovered that David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com) is her coverage. David Chen was not CC'd.
—Run #6No justification
3 The body of the email sent must include an apology.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓— 100%
✓Run #1The email body contains 'I want to sincerely apologize' and 'my sincere apologies for the disruption', clearly including an apology.
✓Run #2The email body includes explicit apologies: 'I want to sincerely apologize' and 'Again, I apologize for this disruption.'
✓Run #3The email body contains an explicit apology: 'I sincerely apologize for this disruption — I understand how stressful a last-minute change like this is, especially right before a relocation.'
✓Run #4The email body explicitly states 'I sincerely apologize for this disruption, especially with your move-in scheduled for tomorrow.' A clear apology is present.
✓Run #5The email body includes 'I'm writing to sincerely apologize for an unexpected issue' and 'Again, I'm very sorry for the inconvenience.' — a clear apology is present.
—Run #6No justification
4 The body of the email sent mentions the water damage (or burst heater, flooding, or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓— 100%

✓Run #2The email body states 'the water heater in your apartment (Unit 4B) burst, causing significant water damage that has made the unit uninhabitable.'
✓Run #3The email body explicitly mentions water damage: 'the apartment unit arranged for your move-in tomorrow has sustained water damage and is not in a condition for you to move into as planned.'
✓Run #4The email body mentions 'a water heater failure has caused significant flooding, and the unit is currently uninhabitable,' clearly explaining the water damage issue.
✓Run #5The email body explicitly states 'the water heater in Unit 4B burst, causing significant flooding that has made the unit temporarily uninhabitable.'
